macro_summary: Tech markets are navigating a post-chip-rout recovery after the Nasdaq-100 fell 1.8% on July 7 amid semiconductor valuation concerns, even as S&P 500 H1 2026 returns of +10.2% and blended Q2 EPS growth of 23.3% YoY support the broader bull case. The Fed holds at 78.1% probability for the July 28-29 meeting, but 9 of 19 officials have penciled in a hike and CPI is running at 4.2% YoY — making FOMC minutes today and the July 14 CPI print twin binary risk events. Big Tech earnings (AAPL, MSFT late July; NVDA August 26) serve as the next major fundamental anchors, with AI infrastructure spending guidance the key variable across all prints.
screening_note: 6 independent screening runs analyzed over the past 24 hours. Minimum threshold set at 2 appearances. All 5 qualifying ideas cleared this bar; 3 achieved perfect 6/6 consensus, representing the strongest overnight conviction.
key_events: FOMC minutes release today July 8 at 2:00 PM ET — hawkish tone risk given 9/19 officials penciled in a hike
SKHY (SK Hynix ADR) Nasdaq debut July 10 — largest ADR listing in history at ~$29.4B
June CPI print July 14 — current YoY run-rate 4.2%, upside surprise reopens rate-hike tail risk
Q2 earnings season kicks off July 13
AAPL fiscal Q3 earnings July 30 — guided 14–17% revenue growth
MSFT fiscal Q4 earnings expected late July — $2.5B Frontier AI deployment business launched July 7
NVDA fiscal Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26; MSFT/GOOG July 29 prints serve as AI capex read-through

ideas.name: SK Hynix Inc. ADR
ideas.thesis: Appeared in all 6 overnight screens with 5 high-conviction votes — the strongest consensus of the digest. SK Hynix's Nasdaq ADR debuts July 10 as the largest ADR listing in history (~$29.4B), removing the structural 'Korea Discount' that has kept US investors locked out of the dominant HBM memory supplier (50–70% market share, order book sold out through 2027). HSBC applied a 38% target upgrade to ₩4M with a 20% P/B premium for improved global accessibility, and cornerstone investors Coatue and Baillie Gifford committed ~$7B, signaling institutional demand; index-fund forced buying post-listing adds a mechanical bid.
ideas.catalyst: Nasdaq ADR first trading day July 10, 2026 — 2 days away
ideas.edge: Trades at ~20.55x P/E vs. US semi peers at 25–35x+; HSBC 38% target uplift on 'Korea Discount' removal; $29.4B listing with ~$7B cornerstone commitments; HBM order book sold out through 2027
ideas.bull_case: Immediate P/E re-rating toward US peer multiples (25–35x) on debut day implies 20–70% upside from listing price; index inclusion flows and institutional accessibility create durable buying pressure beyond first-day pop.
ideas.bear_case: Korean-listed shares already up 250%+ YTD, so much of the re-rating may be pre-priced; first-day ADR pricing could open at a premium to fair value, and options are unlikely available at open — limiting hedging tools and amplifying volatility risk.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: Appeared in all 6 overnight screens with 5 high-conviction votes. Apple's July 30 fiscal Q3 earnings are a hard-dated catalyst backed by concrete guidance of 14–17% revenue growth, following Q2's beat at 17% revenue growth ($111.2B) with iPhone sales +22% YoY. The foldable iPhone buzz (rumored $2,500 price point in a fall 5-device lineup) provides a product narrative overhang, and with AAPL sitting ~$190B below NVDA in market cap, a strong print carries a 'market cap crown' re-rating narrative that could attract incremental momentum buyers. All 6 runs recommend a defined-risk call spread (August expiry) over outright stock given the 37x valuation.
ideas.edge: Guided 14–17% revenue growth with Q2 beat at 17%/$111.2B already in hand; ~$190B market cap gap to NVDA that a strong print could close; 37x earnings multiple with AI narrative multiple-expansion potential
ideas.bull_case: A beat-and-raise on July 30 with foldable iPhone confirmation could compress the $190B gap to NVDA, triggering momentum-driven multiple expansion and index rebalancing flows into the world's second-largest company.
ideas.bear_case: At 37x earnings, AAPL has limited margin for disappointment; any Services growth miss or cautious iPhone cycle commentary (macro FX headwinds, consumer spending) could trigger a sharp de-rating from an elevated multiple.

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in all 6 overnight screens. Microsoft is trading at its cheapest forward multiple (21–22x NTM EPS) since 2023, creating a notable valuation dislocation at a time when 97 analysts maintain bullish consensus and the analyst price target consensus of $589–592 implies approximately 56% upside. The July 7 launch of a $2.5B 'Frontier' AI deployment business and 4,800 job cuts (margin signal) set up a positive cost narrative into fiscal Q4 earnings expected late July. All 6 runs recommend a defined-risk call spread given AI capex headwinds and rate uncertainty.
ideas.catalyst: MSFT fiscal Q4 FY2026 earnings expected late July 2026; FOMC minutes today July 8 as near-term macro read-through
ideas.edge: 21–22x NTM forward P/E — cheapest since 2023; analyst consensus PT ~$589–592 implying ~56% upside; Wolfe Research sees 34% upside; 4,800 job cuts reduce cost overhang ahead of print
ideas.bull_case: Azure AI acceleration plus job-cut-driven margin improvement could re-rate MSFT toward its historical forward multiple range, with a 97-analyst bullish consensus providing a durable floor and limiting downside on any print miss.
ideas.bear_case: Ongoing AI infrastructure capex (data centers, GPU procurement) continues to pressure free cash flow, and a hawkish FOMC minutes tone today could compress tech multiples broadly before earnings, negating the valuation gap thesis near-term.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 4 of 6 overnight screens, all with medium conviction, establishing it as the consensus macro hedge vehicle. The Nasdaq-100 fell 1.8% July 7 on a semiconductor rout (semis -4.5%+), and two binary macro catalysts arrive within 6 days: FOMC minutes today at 2:00 PM ET and CPI on July 14. With CPI at 4.2% YoY and 9 of 19 Fed officials penciling in a hike, a hawkish surprise in either event could extend tech weakness before the earnings season tailwind kicks in. Screeners are split between a put spread hedge and a long call spread — the dominant recommendation leans toward a put spread to protect tech-heavy exposure at defined cost.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC minutes today July 8 at 2:00 PM ET; June CPI July 14, 2026; Q2 earnings season opens July 13
ideas.edge: Semis down 4.5%+ July 7 creates near-term dislocation; 78.1% hold probability limits upside rate shock but hawkish minutes could surprise; 21.9% residual hike odds + 4.2% CPI = asymmetric tail risk for Nasdaq-100 into CPI print
ideas.bull_case: S&P 500 Q2 blended EPS growth of 23.3% YoY and a neutral-to-dovish FOMC tone today could reverse the chip rout quickly, making a call spread on QQQ the better vehicle into earnings season.
ideas.bear_case: If FOMC minutes signal more officials leaning hawkish and July 14 CPI prints above consensus, tech multiples face a double compression event with the Nasdaq-100 re-testing early-July lows — put spread pays off cleanly in this scenario.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: Appeared in 2 of 6 overnight screens at the minimum threshold, representing the lowest consensus in this digest — treat as a speculative swing idea rather than high-conviction. NVDA rebounded July 7 after an AI valuation-driven selloff, with the stock trading near a historically compressed 21.7x forward P/E versus a 5-year average of 72x. The August 26 fiscal Q2 FY2027 earnings are the hard catalyst, with MSFT/GOOG July 29 prints serving as an AI capex read-through that could move NVDA meaningfully ahead of its own report. HBM4 qualification confirmed by Jensen Huang (June 5) supports the Vera Rubin platform cycle narrative. Both runs recommend a September call spread to define risk around the $198–$203 resistance zone.
ideas.catalyst: MSFT/GOOG earnings July 29 as AI capex read-through; NVDA fiscal Q2 FY2027 earnings August 26, 2026
ideas.edge: Forward P/E of 21.7x vs. 5-year avg of 72x; 57-analyst consensus price target ~33% above recent close; Goldman Sachs flagging valuation as 'compelling'; AI capex guided $650B in 2026 rising to $1T in 2027
ideas.bull_case: A historically compressed valuation (21.7x vs. 72x avg) combined with $1T AI capex trajectory and HBM4/Vera Rubin cycle provides a powerful re-rating setup; MSFT/GOOG confirming strong AI infrastructure spend July 29 could front-run the August 26 print aggressively.
ideas.bear_case: The early-July selloff was valuation-driven amid concerns that AI capex could moderate or shift to custom silicon (TPUs, Trainium), and the $198–$203 resistance zone has capped recent rallies — a failed breakout ahead of earnings could trap momentum buyers.
